story_title: Famous People I’ve Met (None of Whom Would Ever Remember Me)
story_summary: The narrator recounts brief encounters with various famous people across decades and the practical, personal lessons gleaned from those meetings.

context.role: KPMG executive (responsible for the firm's Washington, D.C. office)
context.stakes: Personal development and career perspective shaped by brief high-profile encounters

core_conflict: How to interpret and learn from brief encounters with famous people without idolizing them — turning fleeting moments into lasting personal and professional lessons.

decision_moments.trigger: Noticing Margaret Thatcher standing along the perimeter of the crowd, alone except for her bodyguard, at a reception after a concert.
constraints: She was a former prime minister and accompanied by a bodyguard
Event setting was a formal reception
options: Ignore her and remain in my seat
Request an introduction through someone else
decision_moments.reasoning: On an impulse, I walked over and struck up a conversation with her. I’ve been reminded that there is no good reason to be intimidated by anyone.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Potential social embarrassment or awkwardness when approaching a prominent figure
decision_moments.outcome: I spent about twenty minutes talking with her and my self-assurance was reaffirmed by this experience.

decision_moments.trigger: The USM Foundation invited me to accompany them to New York because I had business contacts in the financial community and was an alumnus.
constraints: The visit was a high-profile fundraising outreach involving media and notable attendees
options: Accept the invitation and attend the luncheon
decision_moments.choice: Accept the invitation and attend the luncheon
decision_moments.reasoning: Knowing that I had business contacts in the New York financial community and was an alumnus, they invited me to accompany them.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Minimal social risk; exposure to high-profile public setting
decision_moments.outcome: I had the privilege of sitting at the head table with Miss McCarty and was inspired by her humility and selflessness.

principles.principle: Minor setbacks do not derail long-term career momentum.
principles.evidence: that minor blips do not sidetrack the momentum of a career trajectory.

principles.principle: Serendipity combined with skill can produce transformative opportunities.
principles.evidence: serendipity, coupled with a little skill, can dramatically change one’s life.

principles.principle: Direct engagement with people reduces intimidation and builds confidence.
principles.evidence: I walked over and struck up a conversation with her.

principles.principle: Observe others' grit and sacrifice as motivation to persist in your ambitions.
principles.evidence: He talked about the constant pain, but how it was worth all of it for the glory of Major League baseball.

principles.principle: Humility and selflessness are powerful leadership virtues regardless of fame.
principles.evidence: Even in the midst of the splendor of the New York glitz, her humility and selflessness matched the bright lights of the city.

principles.principle: Family devotion informs leadership priorities and commitment.
principles.evidence: Along with his discourses about political philosophies and personal journeys, his family devotion left a big impression on me

decision_heuristics.heuristic: When you encounter a notable person, consider approaching them directly to have a personal conversation.
decision_heuristics.evidence: I walked over and struck up a conversation with her.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Treat small setbacks as temporary; maintain momentum in your craft and career.
decision_heuristics.evidence: that minor blips do not sidetrack the momentum of a career trajectory.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Prepare your skills and be ready to act when chance presents an opening; serendipity favors the ready.
decision_heuristics.evidence: serendipity, coupled with a little skill, can dramatically change one’s life.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Use examples of others' grit and sacrifice to sustain your own commitment during hardship.

leadership_patterns.risk_tolerance: Willing to take modest interpersonal risks (approaching prominent figures, attending high-profile events) while avoiding high-risk gambles.
leadership_patterns.communication_style: Conversational, curious, and willing to listen more than speak; engages directly when opportunities arise.
leadership_patterns.accountability_posture: Reflective and self-aware; holds self accountable by extracting lessons from experiences.
leadership_patterns.ethical_framing: Values humility, selflessness, and family commitment as central ethical considerations in leadership.

quotes.quote: that minor blips do not sidetrack the momentum of a career trajectory.
serendipity, coupled with a little skill, can dramatically change one’s life.
I walked over and struck up a conversation with her.
there is no good reason to be intimidated by anyone.
